acceptance_criteria.description: Player submits action → narrative advances
Turn includes consequences and choices
Choice selection → narrative consequence
Invalid command → helpful in-character guidance
Character failure → narrative consequence (prompt hardened wave-29)
Story completion → epilogue + archive (LLM epilogue wave-29)
Turn count available in game state
Multiple concurrent sessions (needs session isolation)
Meta-commands do not consume narrative turns
10+ turn session → narrative coherence (needs coherence detector)
Player greeting → Genesis prompt
Complete Genesis → playable world
Post-Genesis references ≥2 established elements (wave-29)
Genre selection → appropriate world
Genesis act state machine (Act I-V modeling)
Repeat Genesis → different phrasing (wave-29)
Default genre available
Terse responses → valid world (wave-29)
Mid-Genesis trait correction (wave-29)
Seamless Genesis→gameplay transition (wave-29)
LLM generates narrative from context
Narrative includes world state references
Tension pacing via 3-act structure (needs pacing system)
Player action reflected in next response
LLM failure → atmospheric in-world fallback (wave-29)
Turn 100+ context ≤500ms (timing added wave-29)
Coherence violation → self-correction (needs detector)
Exploration → 150-300 words + hooks (wave-29)
Streaming pause → suspense (needs streaming infra)
WorldContext provides location, entities, events on query
Cascading state changes propagate to nearby entities (needs world-tick engine)
Diff query returns state changes since last visit
Lazy region generation within 3s (needs on-demand generation)
NPC schedules advance with time (needs world-tick scheduler)
Each region has distinct identity (archetype, atmosphere)
Nearby query < 200ms at 5000 entities (needs Neo4j backend)
World state restored to last completed turn after crash
NPCs have distinct personality traits
Trait evolution after 10+ contrary actions
NPC dialogue reflects personality
Help NPC → relationship increases (wave-29)
NPC dialogue distinct per NPC (wave-29)
NPC hidden goals influence behavior (needs motivation graph)
Companion NPCs → inject relevant scenes
Return after break → NPC references history (wave-29)
NPC beyond knowledge → authentic response (wave-29)
NPC death → emotional cascade (needs motivation graph)
All LLM calls go through unified interface; response envelope includes model, content, usage, latency_ms, cost_usd, and finish_reason
Primary failure triggers fallback; all tiers fail raises AllTiersFailedError; fallback tier recorded in response
Excessive context completes without error; P0 content never truncated; budget computed correctly
Stream buffers token-by-token content; mid-stream error raises cleanly; done event includes total token count
Per-turn costs tracked in response and queryable; session cost cap raises BudgetExceededError to prevent runaway spending
Every LLM call appears in Langfuse with full data; all calls within a turn grouped under one trace (requires live Langfuse integration infra)
Full test suite runs without live LLM calls; mock mode exercises complete call path except HTTP request
End-to-end turn processing: player submits turn → all four pipeline stages execute → narrative streams via SSE; world-state side effects available atomically after generation
Input understanding: intent classified correctly; gibberish → 'other' with low confidence; meta-commands routed separately; LLM failure falls back to keyword detection then 'other'
Context assembly: relevant world state assembled (location, intent, game state); DB unavailability → partial context flag; pipeline still continues with minimal context
Generation quality: narrative produced from assembled context; world-state updates extracted via LLM; suggested actions populated when extraction returns them
Delivery: ThinkingEvent and TurnCompleteEvent SSE types defined with required metadata; deliver_stage marks turn complete; turn persisted to repository
Error resilience: understanding LLM failure → keyword fallback; DB unavailable → minimal context; all LLM tiers fail → in-world fallback narrative; duplicate turns/idempotency keys rejected
Observability: Langfuse per-stage spans with full Understanding and Context objects as attributes (requires live Langfuse integration infra)

specs.title: Prompt & Content Management
sections: Appendix B — Prompt Template Syntax Quick Reference

acceptance_criteria.description: Prompts stored as versioned files; each has unique ID, version, declared variables; system refuses to start if required prompt missing
Registry resolves IDs to active versions; runtime activation/rollback without deploy; events audited
Variables injected correctly; missing required variables raise clear error; sandboxed environment prevents injection
Prompts include shared fragments by reference; circular references detected and rejected
Golden tests detect unintended output changes; scenario tests validate behavioral assertions; deterministic rendering
Genre packs loadable and versioned; include tone, archetypes, location moods, fallback responses
Langfuse generation includes prompt template ID, version, fragment versions; per-version metrics tracked
Every player-facing prompt includes safety preamble; preamble cannot be removed; injection logged observe-only
Authors can preview output; shadow mode processes real inputs without serving players; registration validates syntax
Player can create account, start game, submit turn, receive stream using only documented endpoints
OpenAPI spec passes openapi-spec-validator (requires integration tooling)
Every endpoint returns documented error envelope shape for all error conditions
SSE narrative stream begins delivering chunks within 2 s of turn submission (real-time timing, integration only)
Client reconnect within 30 s receives all missed events (requires Redis pub/sub infra)
Keep-alive heartbeats arrive at least every 15 s during idle (SSE middleware, integration only)
Exceeding turn submission rate limit returns 429 with Retry-After header
Rate-limit headers present on every authenticated response (middleware-level, integration only)
No API response ever contains stack traces, file paths, or internal details
Every error response includes a correlation_id (request_id) traceable in server logs
Unauthenticated requests to protected endpoints return 401, not 403 or 404
Player cannot access another player's game — API returns 404 (not 403)

specs.title: Player Identity & Sessions
specs.implementation_notes: Wave 27: Fixed GameStatus bug — DB insert now uses created (not active), matching spec lifecycle.

acceptance_criteria.description: Anonymous auth returns 201 with player_id, access_token, is_anonymous=true
Upgrade preserves anonymous player_id in token response
Multi-device login: same game list from two browsers
First turn submission transitions game created→active
Paused game resumable within 30-day window (29 days)
Game paused for 31 days found in expired state
Expired game can be resumed with welcome-back narrative
Created game abandoned after 25 hours with no turns
Login locked after 5 failed attempts → 429
Reused refresh token invalidates entire session family
Deleted player credentials rejected at login
No API response contains password or password_hash field
Player data not retrievable via API within 72h of deletion
Deleted player_id cannot be reassigned to new player

specs.title: Persistence Strategy
specs.implementation_notes: Wave 27: AC-12.02 (cache reconstruction), AC-12.05 (Redis latency metrics), AC-12.07 (turn storage latency), AC-12.11 (backup/restore runbook), AC-12.12 (TTL compliance monitoring) implemented. Remaining: AC-12.01/03/04/06/08/09/10 need schema migrations or Neo4j infra.

acceptance_criteria.description: Turn submitted is retrievable from turn history after a server restart
After Redis restart, next turn completes successfully (cache reconstruction transparent)
GDPR deletion request removes all PII from SQL within 72 hours (requires async background job + direct DB query)
Game state after 100 turns matches accumulated effect of all snapshots (no state drift)
Game state read from Redis cache completes in under 5ms p95 (requires real Redis + timing harness)
Cache miss reconstruction from SQL + Neo4j completes in under 500ms p95 (requires real infra)
Turn processing (all storage ops, excluding AI) completes in under 200ms p95 (requires real infra)
World graph traversal (2-hop) completes in under 200ms p95 (requires real Neo4j)
SQL migration applies without manual intervention beyond running the migration command
Neo4j migration script is idempotent (no Neo4j unit-test infra available)
Operator can restore SQL from backup and have service functional within 1 hour (operational procedure)
All Redis keys have a TTL set (no unbounded memory growth)

specs.title: Testing Infrastructure
sections: Appendix B: pytest.ini / pyproject.toml Test Config
specs.implementation_notes: Wave 27: Removed continue-on-error:true from CI integration tests.

specs.title: Data Privacy
sections: Appendix C: v1 vs Future Privacy Maturity

acceptance_criteria.description: Consent required for data processing (wave-29)
Consent categories granular (wave-29)
Consent version tracked (wave-29)
Consent withdrawal possible (wave-29)
LLM provider config documented (wave-29)
Provider change notification logged (wave-29)
Self-hosted Langfuse documented (wave-29)
Age confirmation field (wave-29)
Under-13 blocked (wave-29)
Age gate before data collection (wave-29)
All error responses use standard envelope shape
Correlation IDs flow from request to response to logs
LLM total failure produces friendly player message (502)
Database retries with exponential backoff (wave-30)
Circuit breaker opens after threshold failures
Failed turns don't corrupt game state (turn atomicity)
Concurrent turns for same game rejected with 409
SSE stream delivers error events
Health endpoint reports degraded when non-critical service is down
No stack traces or module paths in production error responses
Empty turn input returns 400 input_invalid (wave-30)
Health endpoint reports unhealthy when Postgres is down
Safe content passes moderation without delay
Harmful content blocked with gentle redirect
Streaming output interrupted within 2 tokens (needs token-level streaming)
Complete audit trail for all moderation actions
System continues serving when moderation is down (fail-open)
Player never sees moderation internals
Prompt injection detected and blocked
Repeated blocks trigger session escalation
Fantasy violence not false-positive blocked
Fail-closed mode blocks all turns when moderation is down
Normal gameplay unaffected by rate limits
429 response with proper headers when limit exceeded
Rate limit headers present on all responses
Concurrent turn submission prevented with 409
Credential stuffing detected and blocked for 15 minutes
In-memory fallback when Redis is down
Active SSE streams not interrupted by rate limiting (needs stream-aware middleware)
Escalating cooldowns for repeated violations
Admin endpoints require valid API key (Bearer token)
Player search by handle returns matching results
Player lookup returns profile, suspension status, game count
GET /admin/games/{id} returns full game state with turns and flags
POST /admin/games/{id}/terminate force-ends active/paused game; 409 if already terminated
POST /admin/moderation/flags/{id}/review marks flag and optionally suspends player
Rate-limit reset clears player counters and IP blocks with audit trail
GET /admin/audit-log returns all write actions with admin_id, action, target, reason, timestamp
Game creation with genesis pipeline
Maximum active game limit enforcement
Game listing with proper sorting and fields
Game resume with context summary
Automatic persistence after each turn
Soft deletion with confirmation
Delete confirmation requirement
Context summary regeneration after absence
Failed persistence recovery
Completed game is read-only
